key_points: Thomas Hall dorm status unclear — students asking if it's still open
Band members may be grouped together in dedicated dorms
Limited firsthand dorm reviews; very little student commentary available
key_quotes: I notice that none of the videos about the dorms at A&M ever mention Thomas Hall (female dorm). Is it still open?
Are band members grouped in the same dorms?

key_points: Party culture is prominent enough that students are warned about over-prioritizing it
Greek life is active with visible stone traditions on campus
Community atmosphere described as exceptionally welcoming, even by outsiders
key_quotes: One day us as a people want be hooked and stuck on parties but more so on changing and taking over the world.
I've NEVER felt so loved. My brothers and sisters treated me like family from the gate.
So next time you're on a HBCU campus you should not walk on Greek Stones if you are not a member of that organization.

key_points: No student reviews of dining halls found in any source
Zero specific complaints or praise about campus food
Prospective students and parents did not raise dining as a concern
key_quotes: No relevant quotes available from the data.

key_points: Student was killed near campus, raising safety-related stress concerns
No mention of counseling services or mental health resources in any source
Safety anxiety expressed directly by a parent: 'You can't even send your daughter to college'
key_quotes: You can't even send your daughter to college! Praying for her family as I know her mother is devastated.
Our students are not safe.
Students rights now!

key_points: Scholarship process is opaque — students told to just call admissions
International transfer students unsure when or if they receive merit aid
Large scholarships (up to $68k over 4 years) are available and attract students from out of state
key_quotes: The enrollment fee payment is required for the transfer merit scholarship referral.
Anyone heard from Alabama A&M University after applying for their presidential scholarship through their website? You might need to call the admissions office.
My family wants me to go to AAMU cause I got a 68k scholarship there for 4 years.

key_points: ABET-accredited engineering programs confirmed, a meaningful credential
Professors are accessible — students explicitly advised to build relationships with them
Very little detailed academic commentary; curriculum depth is largely undiscussed
key_quotes: By going to class you build relationships with the professors. You may need them in the future for recommendations or networking.
AAMU's program is ABET-accredited and just offers a lot.
I can't find anything about AAMU's program or if they also offer a guaranteed internship.

key_points: Scholarship inquiries go unanswered — students told to call rather than receive proactive updates
International students report confusion about scholarship notification timelines
No student commentary praising administrative responsiveness
key_quotes: You might need to call the admissions office.

key_points: Campus is in Huntsville, Alabama — a mid-sized city with off-campus safety concerns
A student was shot and killed at a nearby off-campus apartment complex
Virtual tour exists but students want an in-person walking tour option
key_quotes: Man, I have been looking for this for weeks. I would love to do a walking tour though.
Going to a HBCU is the best thing I ever did in my life because they will take good care of you.
Horrible! You can't even send your daughter to college!

key_points: No alumni career outcome data mentioned by any student or parent
Engineering students comparing AAMU to UAB partly based on internship guarantees UAB offers
ABET accreditation is the main cited career credential
key_quotes: UAB offers a guaranteed internship, and from my time talking to one of the students there, have a good program.
I can't find anything about AAMU's program or if they also offer a guaranteed internship.
Is UAB ABET accredited? Given that, I'd think beyond the guaranteed internship as relatively unimportant — total cost to you, fit, career outcomes matter more.

key_points: $68k four-year scholarship packages reported — strong financial draw for out-of-state students
Students weighing AAMU against in-state options primarily on cost grounds
HBCU experience described as uniquely valuable in ways beyond tuition dollars
key_quotes: My family wants me to go to AAMU cause I got a 68k scholarship there for 4 years.
Going to a HBCU is the best thing I ever did in my life.
I qualify for Hope and the maximum amount of student aid from FAFSA.

Party culture is real and students are actively warned to stay focused
Outsiders who attended describe feeling immediately embraced and welcomed

HBCU community culture creates unusually strong belonging even for non-Black students
New band director signals fresh energy and active recruitment happening now
